prompt:
User: Are a lot of people still against same sex marriages?
Assistant: Yes, many people are still against same-sex marriages. Social conservatives argue that marriage is a fundamental building block of civilization and should remain between one man and one woman. They worry that changing the traditional definition of marriage will have wide-ranging cultural and religious repercussions and may cause harm to society. In many places around the world, same-sex marriages are not legally recognized. In the United States, a number of states have voted to ban same-sex marriages and other states have not yet recognized them.
